share_basis_note: Post-split (20:1, 2022-05) basis. 10,874M diluted weighted shares (Q1 2026) x $244.39 (2026-06-18 close, last <= as_of) = ~$2.66T. Price from Yahoo chart API [S004]; 2026-06-19 not used as freeze edge.

module_signals.finding: Anchor quarter (Q1 2026, ended 2026-03-31) and full-year FY25 baseline are fully primary-sourced from SEC 8-K Ex99.1 (PR + financial statements, saved locally): revenue $181.5B (+17%), AWS $37.6B (+28%), total OI $23.9B (record 13.1% margin), net income $30.3B (incl $16.8B Anthropic pretax gain), TTM OCF $148.5B, TTM FCF $1.2B, Q1 capex $44.2B, LT debt $119B, Q2 guide $194-199B. Price locked from a dated daily series. Material completeness high on current facts; gaps (capex maintenance/growth split + ROIC, 10-K line items, proxy, Anthropic accounting, 10-year series) are haircuts/cap items.

Three-engine compounder: NA+International retail/logistics/ads (margins at cyclical record, NA OI +43% Y/Y), AWS cloud (profit core, ~57-59% of total OI, reaccelerated to +28% / 15-quarter high), and embedded high-margin advertising. The 2023 blind-case thesis (retail-margin trough + AWS durability + cost discipline) has LARGELY PLAYED OUT. The new theme is a ~$200B/yr AI capex bet, predominantly AWS, financed by all of OCF plus added leverage. Business durability high; the capex ROI is the open question.

AWS captures the Western #2 cloud profit pool (FY25 OI $45.6B, ~35-38% margin) via scale + switching costs + full-stack (incl. self-designed Trainium reducing NVDA dependence); retail captures scale economies + Prime flywheel. Moat strong and arguably widening (AWS reaccel + own silicon + Anthropic positioning). Operator grade 4/5: Jassy (AWS architect) CEO + Bezos exec chair, one-share-one-vote (clean governance), strong long-term reinvestment record. Durability HIGH for cloud demand; the ~$200B capex ROIC is undimensioned (O4). Not a bottleneck monopoly (cloud oligopoly), so no bottleneck premium in valuation.

Owner earnings bridge DOES NOT CLOSE this pass (opposite of NVDA, same disease as GOOGL but more extreme). Business is highly profitable (OI $80B FY25, OCF $148.5B TTM) but TTM FCF is ~$1.2B because capex ($131.8B FY25 / $151B TTM / ~$200B 2026 plan) exceeds OCF. Normalized owner earnings depends on the maintenance-vs-growth capex split and the incremental ROIC of the AI buildout (O4, no primary). GAAP net income ($30.3B Q1) is inflated by $16.8B non-operating Anthropic gains -> cannot use GAAP EPS for valuation. Balance sheet shifting from fortress to leveraged-investment mode: LT debt +$53B in Q1 to $119B; net cash (financial debt only) still positive ~+$24B. Survivable but elasticity being consumed.

Kill paths real but not structural impairment at as-of: (1) ~$200B/yr AI capex ROIC < cost of capital -> depreciation wave + interest erode owner earnings = value destruction (F1, the ONLY path that could escalate to veto, but needs years of evidence; O4 undimensioned); (2) AWS share eroded by Azure(OpenAI)/GCP + customer self-silicon + portable AI workloads (F2); (3) record 13.1% retail margin reverts (F3); (4) leverage + rate/demand double-hit (F4, but OCF $148.5B services it); (5) GAAP distortion from Anthropic fair-value swings (F5). NONE is permanent profit-pool loss or balance-sheet death now. The binding issue is VALUATION (no MOS) which CAPS the verdict, not a hard veto. Not a value trap; it is a fully-to-over-priced quality asset.

At ~$2.66T, trailing GAAP P/E ~34x (understated by equity gains), P/FCF meaningless (FCF near zero), starting normalized owner-earnings yield only 2.1-2.5%, price ~11% off 52-week high (upper-middle of range, sentiment bullish). Even on the GENEROUS NOPAT lens (gives capex credit, assumes FCF releases): base 5y IRR ~-1.5%, bull +7.7% (<8% hurdle); conservative OE lens worse (base -6%). Asymmetry skews DOWN. Price is the binding constraint (opposite of NVDA, same as GOOGL). buy-below ~$175-180 for base IRR to reach 8%. No position at current price; WATCH with a STARTER upgrade line.

starter_zone: No starter at current price. STARTER becomes available only at/below ~$175 (base 5y IRR back to the 8% hurdle, near the 52-week low) OR if the AI capex begins releasing FCF / its incremental ROIC is demonstrated.
add_zone: n/a at WATCH. After an upgrade to STARTER (price <= ~$175 or capex-ROI confirmation), scale on confirmation.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: No-chase at and above the current ~$244 (base IRR negative). Watch zone ~$180-195. The price embeds many years of high compounding plus a sustained premium exit multiple at a starting yield of ~2%.
binding_constraint: Price has no margin of safety (starting normalized owner-earnings yield 2.1-2.5%, base 5y IRR negative even giving the ~$200B AI capex full credit) PLUS the owner-earnings bridge does not close (capex maintenance/growth split + incremental ROIC undimensioned, O4). Valuation caps the verdict at WATCH; this is GOOGL-structured (price-capped), the OPPOSITE of NVDA (price-friendly, completeness-capped). Business quality is NOT the constraint.
kill_criteria: Capex keeps surging multiple quarters but incremental AWS revenue/profit does not match it (ROIC deteriorating) -> F1 value destruction. [ONLY path that can escalate toward veto]
AWS growth structurally falls below ~15% with weakening share -> moat/profit-pool erosion (F2).
Total operating margin reverts sharply from the record 13.1% to <9% -> retail-margin durability disconfirmed (F3).
Net financial debt deteriorates while OCF growth stalls -> balance-sheet elasticity exhausted (F4).
Price falls below ~$175 (base 5y IRR back to 8% hurdle) -> UPGRADE: lift the WATCH cap, evaluate STARTER.
Anthropic fair-value swings reverse and crater GAAP net income -> valuation-optics event (non-operating, monitor) (F5).
runner_dissent: Recorded per protocol. The mechanical result (WATCH) feels CORRECT, not wrong: this is a textbook good-business-wrong-price, structurally identical to the GOOGL pilot that the same five-soul panel also put at WATCH. Two honest counter-risks are logged. (1) too_small_missed_asymmetry (the SNDK lesson the harness was built to fix): if the ~$200B/yr AI capex turns out to be another AWS-style contrarian winner that releases enormous FCF in 3-5 years, a WATCH at $244 could miss it. This is exactly why the verdict is WATCH and NOT REJECT, with an explicit STARTER upgrade line (price <= ~$175 OR capex ROIC confirmation / FCF release) -- the framework does not over-veto a genuine compounder; it waits for either price or proof. (2) The owner-earnings bridge does not close (O4): normalized OE is a band ($45-67B), not a point, so base IRR could be better OR worse than -1.5%. But BOTH the generous NOPAT lens and the conservative OE lens point to the same conclusion (price is expensive, starting yield ~2%), so WATCH is robust to the uncertainty -- only the precision is limited. Net: the constraint is price + an unclosable capex/owner-earnings bridge, which CAPS the verdict at WATCH (not a veto: business intact, balance sheet survivable). Unlike NVDA, price is NOT friendly here -- starting yield 2.1-2.5% vs NVDA 5.1%, FCF near zero vs NVDA clean FCF=OE. Lower-confidence items left OPEN: $200B capex maintenance/growth split + ROIC (O4, blocking), 10-K line items (O1), proxy (O3), Anthropic accounting (O6), backlog/Trainium primary (O5).
